guide_response.verdict: AUTHORIZED — 5-min rollback-readiness pre-flight check for EA-9 to run BEFORE fleet pause (Pre-Flight Step 0). Verifies the rollback parachute (`pre-platform-extract` tag + bundle + OOG zip + Phase C fix in devplatform) is intact AHEAD OF irreversible-deletion items 5.1, 5.5, 5.5.v2. Total runtime ~30 seconds. If any check fails, EA-9 STOPS and reports to LA BEFORE any branch checkout, state mutation, or fleet pause. Folds into EA-9 init pointer §Pre-Flight as new sub-step "Step 0 — Rollback Readiness Verification" (BEFORE the existing state.pause_fleet step).
guide_response.purpose:
Per LA directive 2026-04-29, decline a 4th pre-flight audit pass (diminishing returns; the dual-track at 5.0.v2 already caught the Class A n1(e) catastrophe) in favor of a quick rollback-readiness check.
Stage 5 has multiple irreversible deletions (Items 5.1 + 5.5 + 5.5.v2). The rollback escape route per dispatch line 268 + RECOVERY.md is: `git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI reset --hard pre-platform-extract` Plus deeper-recovery via: - Bundle restore from `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\BlarAI_pre_extract.bundle` (full git history backup, 446,552,451 bytes; SHA256 in Stage 0 manifest) - OOG (out-of-git) zip restore from `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\blarai_oog_20260424_171721.zip` (untracked artifacts at Stage 0 baseline, 60,366,033 bytes; SHA256 in Stage 0 manifest)
If any of those rollback assets are missing, corrupted, or moved, the rollback escape route is broken — and EA-9 must NOT proceed with destructive Stage 5 work until the parachute is repacked.
This check verifies (5 critical items, ~30s total runtime): 1. Git tag `pre-platform-extract` exists on BlarAI (rollback target) 2. Bundle file exists at recorded backup path 3. OOG zip exists at recorded backup path 4. Bundle SHA256 matches Stage 0 manifest (no silent corruption) 5. devplatform git log includes Phase C wake_launcher fix at `be22a15` (so rollback to `pre-platform-extract` doesn't lose Phase C remediation on devplatform side; without `be22a15`, rolled-back devplatform would exhibit BUG 1 again — wake_launcher reads stale BlarAI state.json)
guide_response.fold_in_location:
EA-9 init pointer Pre-Flight section. Insert as "Step 0 — Rollback Readiness Verification" BEFORE the existing Step 1 (fleet pause via `state.pause_fleet(..., path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')`).
Rationale for Step 0 placement (before fleet pause): - This check is read-only and informational — no state mutation - If it fails, EA-9 has NOT yet paused the fleet, NOT yet checked out a branch, NOT yet edited any file - Failure leaves the system completely clean; LA can investigate without rollback complications (no fleet pause to revert, no branch state to undo) - PASS authorizes EA-9 to proceed to Step 1 (existing fleet pause SOP)
Step renumbering in EA-9 init pointer: Pre-Flight: Step 0 — Rollback Readiness Verification (NEW; this dispatch) Step 1 — state.pause_fleet on devplatform + commit Step 2 — git status check + V0 pickup verification Step 3 — iter-1 comprehension gate emission ... (existing sequence)

consolidated_powershell_block.description: Run all 5 checks sequentially. Total runtime ~30 seconds. Output is human-readable PASS/FAIL summary with explicit failure list. EA-9 captures the output text in the Stage 5 close STATUS append (Item 5.12).

check.name: pre-platform-extract tag exists on BlarAI
check.failure_disposition: STOP. Tag missing means rollback target is undefined. Investigate via `git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI reflog | Select-String 'pre-platform-extract'` to see if tag was deleted. Recreate from commit `d0dc1c7` (per Stage 0 ledger row in STATUS.md) via: `git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI tag pre-platform-extract d0dc1c7 -m "BlarAI pre-platform-extract baseline (recreated per rollback-readiness check)"`. LA decision required before recreating.
check.citation: STATUS.md Stage Ledger Stage 0 row + Recovery Anchors table; tag object `ce2ec58` on commit `d0dc1c7`.

check.name: Git bundle exists at backup path
check.failure_disposition: STOP. Bundle is the full-git-history backup independent of the local repo. Without it, rollback degrades from "tag + bundle dual recovery" to "tag-only" (single point of failure). Investigate: was bundle deleted, moved, or never created? If genuinely lost, regenerate from current state via: `git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI bundle create C:\Users\mrbla\backups\BlarAI_pre_extract.bundle pre-platform-extract` BEFORE proceeding (regenerated bundle is functionally equivalent but NOT byte-identical to Stage 0 baseline; SHA256 will differ — Check 4 will then mismatch by design).
check.citation: STATUS.md Recovery Anchors table — file size 446,552,451 bytes.

check.name: OOG zip exists at backup path
check.failure_disposition: STOP. OOG (out-of-git) zip captures untracked artifacts at Stage 0 baseline (configs, secrets, runtime files not in git history — e.g., the .mcp.json + state.json snapshots). Without it, rollback recovers git state but NOT non-git state. Investigate: was zip deleted/moved? If genuinely lost, the rollback path becomes "git-only" (degraded — LA decides whether to proceed with degraded rollback or recreate OOG zip first via fresh oog snapshot of current state).
check.citation: STATUS.md Recovery Anchors table — file size 60,366,033 bytes.

check.name: Bundle SHA256 matches Stage 0 manifest
check.expected: True (SHA256 byte-for-byte match against Stage 0 manifest)
check.failure_disposition: STOP. SHA mismatch means bundle was modified or corrupted post-Stage-0 OR was regenerated (per Check 2 failure_disposition fallback). Even if file exists, contents may not be the pristine pre-platform-extract state. Investigate: when did bundle last verify-good? Check filesystem-level metadata (Get-ItemProperty) for last-modified time. If genuinely corrupted, restore from offsite backup if available; if regenerated post-Check-2-failure, accept SHA mismatch as expected and document. LA decision required.
check.citation: STATUS.md Recovery Anchors table — SHA256 column for bundle.

check.name: devplatform Phase C wake_launcher fix at be22a15 in git log
check.expected: Returns line: `be22a15 fix(stage4-remediation): wake_launcher reads devplatform state.json + logs to devplatform`
check.failure_disposition: STOP. If `be22a15` is not in devplatform git log, the Phase C wake_launcher path-resolution fix is not durably committed on devplatform side. Rolling BlarAI back to `pre-platform-extract` would leave devplatform in a state where wake_launcher exhibits BUG 1 (reads BlarAI's stale state.json) AND BUG 4 (writes logs to BlarAI/tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/). Investigate via `git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform reflog | Select-String be22a15` to see if commit was reset/dropped. If genuinely missing, the fix must be re-applied to wake_launcher.ps1 (lines 69 + 809 → $PSScriptRoot derivation) and re-committed BEFORE Stage 5 proceeds.
check.citation: STATUS.md Stage 4 Closure Footnotes §4 (Phase C Remediation) + Anomaly A19 (BUG 1 root cause + fix).

optional_extensions.intro: NOT required for the 5-min minimum check, but mature-not-minimal extensions if EA-9 has time. Each adds ~1-20s to total Step 0 runtime.

ext.name: OOG zip SHA256 verification
ext.citation: STATUS.md Recovery Anchors table — OOG zip SHA256 column.

ext.name: Scheduled task XML backup directory exists with 13 files
ext.expected: True (13 XML files preserved at Stage 0 — full pre-cutover task definition snapshot)
ext.citation: STATUS.md Recovery Anchors table — Scheduled task XMLs entry.

ext.name: devplatform branch is on main
ext.citation: devplatform invariant — work happens on main per platform-separation v2 plan; branching off main during Stage 5 would complicate rollback.

guide_response.success_criteria: All 5 critical checks return PASS. EA-9 proceeds to Step 1 (state.pause_fleet on devplatform + commit) per existing Pre-Flight sequence. No artifact emission needed at this gate; the inline PowerShell output is the audit trail. EA-9 captures the output text block in the Stage 5 close STATUS append (Item 5.12 — under a "Pre-Flight Verification Outputs" subsection).
guide_response.failure_handling:
Any single check FAIL = HARD STOP. EA-9 reports to LA via chat with: - Which check(s) failed (1-5; multiple possible) - Failure detail (output of the check command) - Per-check failure_disposition guidance (quote from this dispatch)
EA-9 does NOT proceed to Step 1 (fleet pause). System remains untouched; LA investigates without rollback complications.
The failure_disposition guidance for each check is in the per_check_details section above. EA-9 may quote that guidance verbatim to LA but does NOT autonomously execute the recovery (e.g., regenerating a missing bundle, recreating a deleted tag, re-applying a missing Phase C fix) — those are LA decisions because they involve modifying baseline state.
guide_response.forward_action_for_guide_9:
Fold this dispatch into the EA-9 init pointer Pre-Flight section as "Step 0 — Rollback Readiness Verification" with the consolidated PowerShell block embedded inline. Reference this artifact for full per-check details and failure dispositions: `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/5.0-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_8_ROLLBACK_READINESS_PREFLIGHT_FOR_EA_9.xml`
Step 0 placement is BEFORE Step 1 (state.pause_fleet) so that a failure leaves the system clean (no fleet pause to revert, no branch checkout to undo). EA-9's first action in Stage 5 execution is therefore: (1) run Step 0 (this dispatch); (2) if PASS: proceed to Step 1 (existing fleet pause SOP); (3) if FAIL: STOP and report per failure_handling above.
Bind this addition under g9-ea9_n1 with a new sub-clause `n1(n)` (or next available letter if g9-ea9_n1 has grown via your 4-correction re-emission of the audit checkpoint verdict — check the corrected sub-clause count first). Suggested binding text:
"n1(n) Pre-Flight Step 0 — rollback-readiness verification per Guide-#8 dispatch 5.0-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_8_ROLLBACK_READINESS_PREFLIGHT_FOR_EA_9.xml. Runs BEFORE state.pause_fleet (Step 1). 5 critical checks (~30s runtime). HARD STOP on any failure; LA decision required for recovery (regenerate tag/bundle/OOG-zip/Phase-C-fix). EA-9 captures output in Stage 5.12 STATUS append."
Cumulative-ack chain at EA-9 spawn becomes 116 + 14 = **130 binding atoms** (was 129 with 13 sub-clauses; n1(n) adds 1).
Optionally, add ext1-ext3 (OOG zip SHA + scheduled-task XML count + devplatform-on-main) as further mature-not-minimal extensions IF EA-9 wall-clock budget permits. Adds ~15-25s to Step 0 runtime; same HARD STOP discipline.

relationship_to_existing_artifacts.artifact: Audit checkpoint covers spec-vs-disk drift detection across all 16 Stage 5 sub-items. This dispatch is COMPLEMENTARY: covers backup/recovery asset integrity, which is OUT OF SCOPE for the audit checkpoint (which audited forward-state, not rollback-state).

Handoff XML watchpoints w8/dn10 cover wake_launcher auto-stash discipline (don't pop) per A20 correction. This dispatch covers a different rollback- adjacent surface: Stage 0 backup integrity + Phase C remediation durability on devplatform side.

Dispatch's rollback prescription is `git reset --hard pre-platform-extract`. This Step 0 verifies the prerequisite of that rollback being viable. Without Step 0, the dispatch's rollback is structurally promised but not operationally verified at execution time.
